entry.title: Re: How to fill item navigation property of standard API...
entry.content:
Hi VB_developer,

your request should look similar like the following that i used to call the Salesorder A2X API:  

    DATA deep_busi_data TYPE tys_a_sales_order_simulation_t.
    DATA error_message TYPE string.

    deep_busi_data    =    VALUE #(
                       sales_order_type            = 'OR'
                       sales_organization          = '1010'
                       distribution_channel        = '10'
                       organization_division       = '00'
                       sold_to_party               = '10100001'
                       purchase_order_by_customer  = 'SalesOrder Simulation'
                       to_item = VALUE #( (
                          sales_order_item = '1'
                          material = 'TG11' "'TG122'
                          requested_quantity = '5'
                          ) )
                       ).


    DATA(response) = simulatesalesorder(
                       EXPORTING
                         i_salesorderdata = deep_busi_data
                       IMPORTING
                         r_error_message  = error_message
                     ).

 

Where the type is a deep structure as follows:

 TYPES:
      "! <p class="shorttext synchronized">A_SalesOrderSimulationType</p>
      BEGIN OF tys_a_sales_order_simulation_t,
        "! <em>Key property</em> SalesOrder
        sales_order                TYPE c LENGTH 10,
        "! SalesOrderType
        sales_order_type           TYPE c LENGTH 4,
        "! SalesOrganization
        sales_organization         TYPE c LENGTH 4,
        "! DistributionChannel
      ....
        customer_payment_terms     TYPE c LENGTH 4,
        "! PaymentMethod
        payment_method             TYPE c LENGTH 1,

        to_item                    TYPE tyt_a_sales_order_item_simul_2,

      END OF tys_a_sales_order_simulation_t,
      "! <p class="shorttext synchronized">List of A_SalesOrderSimulationType</p>
      tyt_a_sales_order_simulation_t TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF tys_a_sales_order_simulation_t WITH DEFAULT KEY.

entry.title: Using the Employee Central Assignment ID as Personnel Number (PERNR) in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

We are currently implementing S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (S4C) and Successfactors Employee Central (SF). The automatic replication of employees from SF to S4C is part of this.

We would like to have the personnel number of a Worker in S4C (Work Agreement field Personnel Number) to be the same as the number in SF, the SF Assignment ID / User ID field.

According to note DIGITS this should be possible, because the prerequisites are fulfilled:

* We have not yet activated the employee replication from SF to S4C for any SF and S4 system.

* And we have activated the Assignment ID in SF.

But I wonder if it will remain possible to create Workers also manually after the feature to copy the personnel number from SF is activated. Is this still possible?

The situation in our company is that we have both internal employees and external (contingent) workers.

We are a recruitment and secondment agency for SAP consultants hiring external workers on an individual basis for long-term assignments at customers. So these people are no employees of our company but only workers which we hire and rehire to customers through S4C Professional Services customer projects and purchase orders. They should not be part of Successfactors.

I know that Successfactors also has functionality to define contingent employees but for these people this brings unnecessary additional license costs. We do not need any SF functionality for these people. They only have to enter their working hours in the S4C timesheet weekly.

So the question is, can we activate the assignment ID replication from SF to S4C and still create external workers manually directly in S4C without running into problems?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Using the Employee Central Assignment ID as Personnel...
entry.content:
For the First question,

The Employee Central Assignment Id cannot be equivalent to the Personnel number in S/4HANA. This functionality is not valid from this latest release.

For the second question,

Even if you are creating the Employee via any integration, it will still be possible to create the contingent worker manually via the Manage Workforce application.

 

Thanks and Regards,
Moumita.

entry.title: Where can I start learning the basic configuration settings for billing documents in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring Billing in SAP S/4HANA Sales learning journey.

Overview
In this learning journey, you can learn the basic configuration settings for billing documents in SAP S/4HANA. Different variants and ways of creating billing documents are also discussed. As well as special processes like working with down payments and installment payments.

Learning objectives
This learning journey will prepare you to:

  • Configure organizational units for billing
  • Configure billing types
  • Use special billing types
  • Configure the data flow for billing
  • Create billing documents in various ways
  • Configure account determination
  • Configure the interface between Sales and Distribution and Financial Accounting

Prerequisites

  • Applying SAP S/4HANA Sales

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP Green Ledger Is Out Now
entry.content:
SAP is committed to reduce its net carbon emissions by 2030.  

Sustainability is one of the top-of-mind topics in today’s world. Embark with us on the journey towards a more sustainable future with the SAP Green Ledger! We’re happy to announce the General Availability (GA) and first public release of the SAP Green Ledger solution. 

The SAP Green Ledger is hosted on the SAP Business Technology Platform and combines your financial accounting with GHG emissions quantities for your business. By applying the financial principles of double-entry accounting to carbon costs, it provides a clear and quantifiable understanding of the carbon impact, from provisioning to delivery. It supports you in defining targeted emissions reduction actions to be taken at specific business units, cost centers, departments, locations, profit centers, or segments. It also provides you with auditable reports regarding sustainability standards and regulations. 

Let’s take a closer look at our vision with this video.

 

 

 

 

 

 

The SAP Green Ledger solution comes with the following key capacities: 

Support for integration with other systems 

The broad benefits of this solution come in part from its ability to integrate with other systems. Among them are the following: 

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA 
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (this is optional, but it facilitates importing carbon data into SAP Green Ledger) 
  • SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud (this is optional, but it allows for better reporting and analysing of carbon data) 

Replication of corporate financial master data 

You can import corporate master data from connected business systems like SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, thus aligning carbon accounting with the global corporate financial framework. 

Carbon data collection and posting 

SAP Green Ledger is the central hub for the accounting of greenhouse gas emissions data incurred by a company through its activities or its purchased goods and services. You can import this data seamlessly from SAP Sustainability Footprint Management import it from external sources by uploading spreadsheets, or enter it manually, and later post it to the journal at the entity level. 

Carbon data export to group reporting 

You can export carbon data from SAP Green Ledger to SAP Group Reporting Data Collection. From there, your data is mapped and imported to group reporting inSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The data is consolidated with financial data for group reporting and analysis. 

Allocation management 

You can allocate posted carbon quantities to the specific cost centers, profit centers, or accounts responsible for those emissions. This lets you quantify the global carbon and financial cost for specific departments, functional areas, locations, profit centers, segments, or units in your company. 

Visualization and reporting 

Thanks to SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud, you can visualize the data in multiple ways and create audit-quality reports to support data-driven decision makin This integration allows you to analyse your carbon data in comparison to your financial data. It allows for analytics on financial dimension in relation to financial figures, such as revenues, margins, or income. 

Additionally, you can create internal reports and use them to further analyse your carbon data within SAP Green Ledger. 

GHG Emission Planning 

You can use your carbon data to provide actuals for GHG emission planning Based on these actuals emissions and their financial impact can be planned and simulated for future fiscal periods. For more information, see Blog Post: Driving Corporate Sustainability. 

For more information regarding features and functions, see the Feature Scope Description. 

SAP Green Ledger is your ticket towards more sustainability. Why don’t you join us on our sustainability journey? Learn more about SAP Green Ledger today!

entry.title: Customer Return Invoice Request with status With Conflict
entry.content:
The Customer Return Invoice Request with status With Conflict

  1. Go to Customer Invoicing - Invoice Requests 
  2. Select Customer Return Invoice Request
  3. The document is with status With Conflict  
  4. Click on Check button 

Conflict in item 10; cancellation requested but invoice already exists

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Customer Return Invoice Request with status With Conf...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

This issue usually happens when the relevant Credit Memo is Released, but the corresponding Customer Return and Inbound Delivery are with Cancelled status. 

Since all the documents are already cancelled the Credit memo should also be cancelled. Due to this system is showing the status of invoice request as With Conflict  and error details "Conflict in item 10; cancellation requested but invoice already exists". 

Regards,

Arjun

entry.title: SAP Router (seclogin: Can't add credentials)
How to Handle Dimension Display Options (ID, Description, ID + Description) in R-Scripting
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using R Visualizations in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and would like to dynamically control how dimension values are displayed on the x-axis based on their Display Options:

  1. ID Only
  2. Description Only
  3. ID and Description

In standard SAC charts, this behavior is handled by setting the display option for the dimension. However, I am not sure how to replicate this functionality in an R Visualization.

For example:

  • Dimension ID: A, B, C
  • Dimension Description: Alpha, Beta, Gamma

I want to visualize these in R with the following behaviors:

  1. X-axis shows ID only (A, B, C).
  2. X-axis shows Description only (Alpha, Beta, Gamma).
  3. X-axis shows both ID and Description combined (A - Alpha, B - Beta, C - Gamma).

Could anyone guide me on how to implement this using R-scripting in SAC? Specifically, how do I map or transform the dimension values to achieve this flexibility in R?

Thank you!
